title: Salty Crunchy Japanese Gyoza
description: Pan-fried Japanese dumplings filled with pork and vegetables, served with a crunchy dill pickle topping.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine ground pork, shredded cabbage, thinly sliced green onion, grated ginger, soy sauce, and sesame oil. Mix well until just combined.
Cover the mixture and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Prepare the dumpling wrappers by cutting the all-purpose flour into small pieces and mixing with a pinch of salt. Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into small balls, about the size of a small egg. Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 3 inches in diameter.
Place a tablespoon of the pork mixture in the center of each dough circle. Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape, and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Heat a large non-stick skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add a small amount of sesame oil to the pan and swirl to coat.
Add the dumplings to the pan and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown and crispy. Repeat with the remaining dumplings.
Serve the gyoza hot with a crunchy dill pickle topping. Garnish with additional green onion and sesame seeds, if desired.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
